title: Is Academy Sports + Outdoors (ASO) Using Analyst Day Access To Reframe Its Long-Term Growth Story?
content:
In early April 2026, Academy Sports + Outdoors announced it will host an Analyst Day on April 7, 2026, followed by CEO and CFO participation in a webcast fireside chat at the J.P. Morgan Retail Roundup Conference in New York on April 8, 2026, with both events available via the company’s investor relations website for about 30 days. Together, these events give investors extended access to senior leadership’s views on long-term strategy and growth initiatives, reinforcing Academy’s emphasis on transparency and more direct engagement with the market. We’ll now examine how Academy’s upcoming Analyst Day, with its focus on long-term strategy and growth initiatives, could influence its investment narrative.

Capitalize on the AI infrastructure supercycle with our selection of the 36 best 'picks and shovels' of the AI gold rush converting record-breaking demand into massive cash flow.

Academy Sports and Outdoors Investment Narrative Recap

To own Academy Sports + Outdoors, you need to believe it can keep growing sales and profits while holding margins in a tough, promotion-heavy retail market. The biggest near term catalyst remains execution on store expansion and omnichannel growth, while a key risk is rising cost pressure from tariffs, labor, and shipping. The newly announced Analyst Day and J.P. Morgan fireside chat do not materially change these near term drivers, but they may shape how investors understand them.

The upcoming Analyst Day on April 7, 2026 is especially relevant here, as management plans to walk through long term strategy and growth initiatives. For investors focused on catalysts like e-commerce growth, private label expansion, and store openings, the detailed presentations and Q&A could clarify how Academy intends to manage margin pressure, capital allocation, and competitive risks, and how that aligns or diverges from current consensus expectations.

Yet while expansion and omnichannel investments are front and center, investors should also be aware of rising promotional intensity and what that could mean for...

Read the full narrative on Academy Sports and Outdoors (it's free!)

Academy Sports and Outdoors' narrative projects $7.2 billion revenue and $460.3 million earnings by 2028. This requires 6.6% yearly revenue growth and a $89.4 million earnings increase from $370.9 million today.

Uncover how Academy Sports and Outdoors' forecasts yield a $59.94 fair value, a 5% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other PerspectivesASO 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Some of the most optimistic analysts were already assuming revenue could reach about US$7.8 billion and earnings around US$498 million, so you should weigh those upbeat expectations against risks like e-commerce competition and consider how the new Analyst Day and conference commentary might push those views higher or lower.

title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Announces Participation in Upcoming Investor Conference
content:
KATY, Texas, April 2, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Academy Sports + Outdoors ("Academy" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: ASO), a leading full-line sporting goods and outdoor recreation retailer, today announced its participation in the upcoming J.P. Morgan Retail Roundup Conference to be held in New York, New York from April 8, 2026 to April 9, 2026. Chief Executive Officer, Steve Lawrence, and Chief Financial Officer, Carl Ford, will participate in a fireside chat that will be webcast live on April 8, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time. The live webcast can be accessed through this link.Academy Sports + Outdoors (PRNewsfoto/Academy Sports + Outdoors)

A live and replay webcast of the fireside chat will be made available for approximately 30 days on the Company's investor relations website at https://investors.academy.com/.

About Academy Sports + Outdoors Academy is a leading full-line sporting goods and outdoor recreation retailer in the United States. Originally founded in 1938 as a family business in Texas, Academy has grown to more than 300 stores across 21 states and counting. Academy's mission is to provide "Fun for All" and Academy fulfills this mission with a localized merchandising strategy and value proposition that strongly connects with a broad range of consumers. Academy's product assortment focuses on key categories of outdoor, apparel, sports & recreation and footwear through both leading national brands and a portfolio of private label brands. For more information, visit www.academy.com.

title: Firearm background checks rise sequentially in March
content:
[Pile of rifle bullets creating a border]

The FBI's National Instant Criminal Background Check System reported on Thursday that firearm background checks rose 13.9% on a sequential basis in March to 2,450,414. Firearm background checks were also higher than the level seen in January. Background checks were down 4.8% on a year-over-year basis. The background checks have correlated closely to actual firearm sales in the past.

The all-time high for firearm background checks was March 2021, when 4,691,738 firearm background checks were done in a single month. FBI firearm background checks then fell progressively later in 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025.

Industry insiders expect firearm demand in 2026 to be broadly similar to 2025, with macro headwinds such as inflation from tariffs, interest rates, and consumer spending pressure as the main risk rather than a collapse in underlying interest.

Stocks that have some connection to firearm demand developments to varying degrees include Smith & Wesson (SWBI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SWBI]), Sturm Ruger (RGR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RGR]), Vista Outdoor, Sportsman's Warehouse (SPWH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPWH]), Ammo (POWW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/POWW]), ammunition manufacturer Olin Corporation (OLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OLN]), outdoors retailer American Outdoor Brands (AOUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AOUT]), hunting gear seller Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DKS]), gunfire locator firm SoundThinking (SSTI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SSTI]), ammunition provider National Presto Industries (NPK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NPK]), taser maker Axon Enterprise (AXON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXON]), and retailer Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASO]).

title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Announces 2026 Analyst Day Event
content:
KATY, Texas, March 31, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Academy Sports + Outdoors ("Academy" or the "Company") (NASDAQ: ASO), a leading full-line sporting goods and outdoor recreation retailer, plans to host an Analyst Day event on April 7, 2026.Academy Sports + Outdoors (PRNewsfoto/Academy Sports + Outdoors)

Beginning at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time, Steve Lawrence, Chief Executive Officer, Carl Ford, Chief Financial Officer, and Chad Fox, Chief Customer Officer, will present and discuss the Company's long-term strategy as well as providing updates on growth initiatives.

A live webcast of the management presentation will be accessible on the Company's website at investors.academy.com from the "News and Events" dropdown menu, under the "Events" tab.

Presentation materials will be posted immediately prior to the event and a replay of the webcast will be archived at investors.academy.com for approximately 30 days.

About Academy Sports + Outdoors Academy is a leading full-line sporting goods and outdoor recreation retailer in the United States. Originally founded in 1938 as a family business in Texas, Academy has grown to more than 300 stores across 21 states and counting. Academy's mission is to provide "Fun for All" and Academy fulfills this mission with a localized merchandising strategy and value proposition that strongly connects with a broad range of consumers. Academy's product assortment focuses on key categories of outdoor, apparel, sports & recreation and footwear through both leading national brands and a portfolio of private label brands. For more information, visit www.academy.com.
